instruction:
The motion of an object is described by the equation $ rac{d
u}{dt} = - k
u^2 t$, where $k$ is a positive constant. When $t=0$, the initial velocity is $
u_{0}$. What is the relationship between velocity $
u$ and time $t$?

Options:
A. $
u = - rac{1}{2}kt^2 + 
u_{0}$
B. $ rac{1}{
u} =  rac{k}{2
u_{0}}t +  rac{1}{
u_{0}}$
C. $
u = - rac{k 
u_{0}^2}{2} + 
u_{0}$
D. $
u =  rac{1}{2}k
u_{0}^2 + 
u_{0}$
E. $
u =  rac{k
u_{0}}{2}t + 
u_{0}$
F. $ rac{1}{
u} = - rac{k}{2
u_{0}}t^2 +  rac{1}{
u_{0}}$
G. $ rac{1}{
u} =  rac{1}{2}ka^2 +  rac{1}{
u_{0}}$
H. $
u =  rac{k
u_{0} t^2}{2} + 
u_{0}$
I. $ rac{1}{
u} = - rac{1}{2}ka^2 +  rac{1}{
u_{0}}$
J. $
u = - rac{k
u_{0}}{2}t^2 + 
u_{0}$
